questions.prompt: Choose the correct indefinite article: Quiero comprar ___ coche.
questions.tricks: Coche is masculine singular → indefinite article is 'un'.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct indefinite article for the noun 'libro' (book).
questions.tricks: Libro is masculine singular → 'un libro'.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct option: ___ agua fría es buena para la salud.
questions.tricks: Agua is feminine but starts with stressed 'a', so it takes masculine article 'el' in singular. This is a special rule!

questions.prompt: ___ manzanas están muy rojas hoy.
questions.tricks: Manzanas is feminine plural → 'Las'.

questions.prompt: Fill in the blank: ___ sol brilla en ___ cielo.
questions.tricks: Both 'sol' and 'cielo' are masculine → 'El sol brilla en el cielo'.

questions.prompt: ___ estudiantes están en clase.
questions.tricks: Estudiantes is masculine plural → 'los'.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct article: ___ águila vuela alto.
questions.tricks: Águila is feminine but begins with stressed 'a', so it takes 'el' in singular. Same rule as agua!

questions.prompt: ___ mesa tiene ___ caramelos.
questions.tricks: Mesa is feminine → 'La'; caramelos is masculine plural → 'los'.

questions.prompt: ___ coche es muy rápido.
questions.tricks: Coche is masculine singular → 'El'.

questions.prompt: ___ mamá y ___ papá están en ___ piso.
questions.tricks: Mamá is feminine → 'la'; papá is masculine → 'el'; piso is masculine → 'el'.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct article: ___ libro
questions.tricks: Libro is masculine singular → indefinite article 'un'.

questions.prompt: 'Las casas en esta calle son muy ____.'
questions.tricks: Adjectives must agree with the noun. 'Casas' is feminine plural → 'blancas'.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct definite article for 'niña'.
questions.tricks: Niña is feminine singular → 'la'.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct indefinite article for 'casas'.
questions.tricks: Casas is feminine plural → 'unas'.

questions.prompt: ___ perros son muy juguetones.
questions.tricks: Perros is masculine plural → 'Los'.

questions.prompt: Necesito ___ bolígrafo para escribir.
questions.tricks: Bolígrafo is masculine singular → indefinite 'un'.

questions.prompt: ¿Dónde está ___ baño?
questions.tricks: Baño is masculine → definite article 'el'.

questions.prompt: ___ leche está fría.
questions.tricks: Leche is feminine → 'La'.

questions.prompt: Compré ___ zapatos nuevos.
questions.tricks: Zapatos is masculine plural → 'unos'.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct article: ___ árbol es muy grande.
questions.tricks: Árbol is masculine singular → 'El'.

questions.prompt: ___ idiomas son difíciles de aprender.
questions.tricks: Idiomas is masculine plural → 'Los'.

questions.prompt: ¿Cuánto cuesta ___ manzana?
questions.tricks: Manzana is feminine singular → indefinite 'una'.

questions.prompt: Tengo ___ hambre terrible.
questions.tricks: Hambre is feminine but starts with stressed 'a' → takes 'un' in singular. Special rule!

questions.prompt: ___ agua del río es muy clara.
questions.tricks: Agua takes 'El' in singular due to stressed 'a' at the start, even though it is feminine.

questions.prompt: Ella es ___ médica muy buena.
questions.tricks: Indefinite article with profession + adjective: 'una médica'.

questions.prompt: ___ lunes tengo clase de español.
questions.tricks: Days of the week used habitually take 'los': 'Los lunes'.

questions.prompt: Vi ___ película muy interesante ayer.
questions.tricks: Película is feminine → indefinite 'una'.

questions.prompt: What is the Spanish subject pronoun for 'they' (mixed or masculine group)?
questions.tricks: Ellos is used for masculine or mixed-gender groups; Ellas is only for all-female groups.

questions.prompt: In Spain, which pronoun is used as the informal plural 'you' for a group of females?
questions.tricks: Vosotras is the feminine form of vosotros, used in Spain for an all-female group.

questions.prompt: Which sentence uses the correct indirect object pronoun? 'I give the book to her.'
questions.tricks: The indirect object pronoun for 'her' is 'le'. 'La' is a direct object pronoun.

questions.prompt: Which sentence correctly uses the indirect object pronoun? 'We tell him the truth.'
questions.tricks: Indirect object pronoun for 'him' is 'le'.

questions.prompt: Which sentence correctly describes what 'you' (tú) think?

choices.text: Tú pensas que el libro es bueno.
Tú piensas que el libro es bueno.
Tú pienso que el libro es bueno.
Tú pensen que el libro es bueno.

questions.answerText: Tú piensas que el libro es bueno.
questions.tricks: The correct stem-changing conjugation of 'pensar' for tú is 'piensas' (e→ie change).

questions.prompt: Yo ___ Dora.
questions.tricks: First person singular of 'ser' is 'soy'.

questions.prompt: Mis amigos ___ muy simpáticos.
questions.tricks: Amigos is third person plural → 'son' (from ser).

questions.prompt: Ella ___ cansada.
questions.tricks: Third person singular of 'estar' is 'está'. Tiredness is a temporary state → use estar.

questions.prompt: What is the subject pronoun for 'I' in Spanish?
questions.tricks: Yo = I (first person singular).

questions.prompt: What is the subject pronoun for 'you' (informal) in Spanish?
questions.tricks: Tú is the informal second person singular subject pronoun.

questions.prompt: Which is the correct direct object pronoun for 'him'?
questions.tricks: Lo is the masculine direct object pronoun for 'him/it'.

questions.prompt: Which pronoun replaces 'a María' in the sentence 'Llamo a María'?
questions.tricks: María is a direct object and feminine → 'la'. 'Llamo a María' → 'La llamo'.

questions.prompt: Complete: '___ gusta el café.' (He likes coffee.)
questions.tricks: Gustar uses indirect object pronouns. Third person singular → 'Le gusta'.

questions.prompt: Which is the reflexive pronoun for 'nosotros'?
questions.tricks: The reflexive pronoun for 'nosotros' is 'nos'.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct pronoun: 'Él ___ habló ayer.' (He spoke to me yesterday.)
questions.tricks: Indirect object 'to me' = 'me'.

questions.prompt: Which sentence is correct? (I wash myself.)
questions.tricks: Reflexive: 'me' for first person. 'Yo me lavo'.

questions.prompt: What does 'os' refer to in Spanish?
questions.tricks: Os is the second person plural informal pronoun used in Spain (vosotros form).

questions.prompt: Choose the correct form: 'She sees ___ every day.' (them, masculine)
questions.tricks: Direct object pronoun for masculine plural = 'los'.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'A nosotros ___ encanta bailar.'
questions.tricks: Indirect object for 'nosotros' = 'nos'.

questions.prompt: What does 'se' indicate in a reflexive sentence?
questions.tricks: Se is the reflexive pronoun for él/ella/usted/ellos/ellas/ustedes.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct sentence: 'I give it (the book, masculine) to you.'
questions.tricks: Double object pronouns: indirect (te) before direct (lo). 'Te lo doy'.

questions.prompt: Which pronoun is used for 'you' (formal, singular) in most Spanish-speaking countries?
questions.tricks: Usted is the formal second person singular, using third-person verb conjugation.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Ellos ___ escribieron una carta.' (They wrote her a letter.)
questions.tricks: Indirect object for 'her' = 'le'. 'Le escribieron una carta'.

questions.prompt: Voy ___ mercado esta tarde.
questions.tricks: 'A + el' contracts to 'al' in Spanish. 'Voy al mercado'.

questions.prompt: El libro está ___ la mesa.
questions.tricks: 'En' is used to indicate location: 'The book is on the table'.

questions.prompt: Vengo ___ España.
questions.tricks: 'De' indicates origin: 'I come from Spain'.

questions.prompt: Voy ___ la tienda ___ comprar leche.
questions.tricks: 'A' indicates direction; 'para' indicates purpose: 'I go to the store to buy milk'.

questions.prompt: Estudio ___ la mañana.
questions.tricks: 'Por' is used with parts of the day: 'por la mañana' (in the morning).

questions.prompt: ¿Cuánto pagaste ___ ese libro?
questions.tricks: 'Por' expresses exchange: 'How much did you pay for that book?'

questions.prompt: Este regalo es ___ ti.
questions.tricks: 'Para' is used to express recipient: 'This gift is for you'.

questions.prompt: La farmacia está ___ el banco.
questions.tricks: 'Al lado de' means 'next to'. The pharmacy is next to the bank.

questions.prompt: Hablan ___ el teléfono.
questions.tricks: 'Por' is used for means of communication: 'They speak by phone'.

questions.prompt: Caminé ___ el parque.
questions.tricks: 'Por' expresses movement through a place: 'I walked through the park'.

questions.prompt: El gato está ___ la caja. (inside)
questions.tricks: 'Dentro de' = inside. 'El gato está dentro de la caja'.

questions.prompt: La lámpara está ___ la mesa. (above)
questions.tricks: 'Encima de' = on top of / above.

questions.prompt: Vivo ___ Madrid.
questions.tricks: 'En' is used for location of residence: 'I live in Madrid'.

questions.prompt: Salgo ___ casa a las ocho.
questions.tricks: 'De' indicates departure from a place: 'I leave from home'.

questions.prompt: El perro está ___ el sofá. (under)
questions.tricks: 'Debajo de' = under/beneath.

questions.prompt: La tienda está ___ el hospital y el banco.
questions.tricks: 'Entre' = between. 'La tienda está entre el hospital y el banco'.

questions.prompt: Estoy aquí ___ ayudarte.
questions.tricks: 'Para' expresses purpose: 'I am here to help you'.

questions.prompt: Viajé ___ tren.
questions.tricks: 'En' is used with modes of transport: 'Viajé en tren'.

questions.prompt: Gracias ___ tu ayuda.
questions.tricks: 'Por' expresses cause/reason: 'Thank you for your help'.

questions.prompt: El cuadro está ___ la pared. (on)
questions.tricks: 'Sobre' = on/upon (for vertical surfaces). 'El cuadro está sobre la pared'.

questions.prompt: Yo ___ en casa. (estar)
questions.tricks: First person singular of 'estar' is 'estoy'. Irregular yo form!

questions.prompt: Tú ___ un pastel delicioso. (hacer)
questions.tricks: Second person singular of 'hacer' is 'haces'.

questions.prompt: Nosotros ___ la tarea juntos. (hacer)
questions.tricks: First person plural of 'hacer' is 'hacemos'.

questions.prompt: 'Yo ___ la tarea en la biblioteca.' (hacer)
questions.tricks: First person singular of 'hacer' is 'hago'. Irregular yo form!

questions.prompt: Nosotros ___ ejercicio por la mañana. (hacer)
questions.tricks: 'Hacemos' is the nosotros form of 'hacer'.

questions.prompt: 'Normalmente, yo ___ la tarea por la noche.'
questions.tricks: Habitual present action → present tense: 'hago'.

questions.prompt: Nosotros ___ terminado el proyecto. (haber)
questions.tricks: 'Hemos' is the present perfect auxiliary for 'nosotros'.

questions.prompt: What is the correct way to say 'there is' or 'there are' in the present tense?
questions.tricks: 'Hay' is the impersonal form of 'haber' for present: 'there is/are'. It never changes form!

questions.prompt: En la fiesta ___ mucha gente.
questions.tricks: Imperfect of 'haber' for past 'there was' is 'había' (always singular).

questions.prompt: Si tuviera dinero, ___ un coche nuevo. (comprar)
questions.tricks: Conditional sentences with 'si + imperfect subjunctive' use the conditional tense: 'compraría'.

questions.prompt: La reunión ___ en la sala principal. (ser)
questions.tricks: 'Ser' is used for scheduled events and their location in formal contexts: 'La reunión es en la sala'.

questions.prompt: Nosotros ___ felices. (ser)
questions.tricks: First person plural of 'ser' is 'somos'.

questions.prompt: Mi profesor ___ muy amable. (ser)
questions.tricks: Permanent traits use 'ser'. Third person singular is 'es'.

questions.prompt: Ella ___ muy inteligente. (ser)
questions.tricks: Permanent characteristic → 'ser'. Third person singular is 'es'.

questions.prompt: Nosotros ___ listos para el examen. (estar)
questions.tricks: Temporary state → 'estar'. First person plural is 'estamos'.

questions.prompt: Esta mañana, antes de salir de casa, yo ya ___ desayunado. (haber)
questions.tricks: Past action completed before another past action uses past perfect (pluperfect): 'había'.

questions.prompt: Which is the correct conjugation of 'poder' for 'nosotros'?
questions.tricks: 'Podemos' — the nosotros form of 'poder'. The o→ue stem change does NOT apply to nosotros/vosotros!

questions.prompt: Complete the sentence: '¿___ tú venir mañana?' (poder)
questions.tricks: Second person singular of 'poder' is 'puedes' (o→ue stem change).

questions.prompt: Choose the correct Spanish sentence for: 'They study a lot.'
questions.tricks: Third person plural of 'estudiar' is 'estudian'.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct Spanish sentence for: 'She is very intelligent.'
questions.tricks: Permanent trait → 'ser'. Correct word order: subject + verb + adverb + adjective.

questions.prompt: Conjugate 'hablar' for 'tú' in the present tense.
questions.tricks: 'Tú hablas' — standard -ar conjugation for second person singular.

questions.prompt: Conjugate 'comer' for 'ellos' in the present tense.
questions.tricks: 'Ellos comen' — third person plural of -er verb 'comer'.

questions.prompt: Conjugate 'vivir' for 'yo' in the present tense.
questions.tricks: 'Yo vivo' — first person singular of -ir verb 'vivir'.

questions.prompt: What is the 'usted' form of 'tener' in the present tense?
questions.tricks: 'Usted tiene' — 'tener' is irregular: yo tengo, tú tienes, él/usted tiene.

questions.prompt: Conjugate 'ir' for 'nosotros' in the present tense.
questions.tricks: 'Nosotros vamos' — 'ir' is highly irregular in the present tense.

questions.prompt: What is the 'yo' form of 'ser' in the present tense?
questions.tricks: 'Yo soy' — 'ser' is irregular: soy, eres, es, somos, sois, son.

questions.prompt: Conjugate 'querer' for 'ella' in the present tense.
questions.tricks: 'Ella quiere' — stem-changing verb (e→ie): quiero, quieres, quiere, queremos, queréis, quieren.

questions.prompt: What is the preterite (past) tense of 'hablar' for 'yo'?
questions.tricks: First person singular preterite of -ar verbs: 'hablé' (note the accent!).

questions.prompt: What is the preterite of 'ir' for 'ellos'?
questions.tricks: 'Ir' and 'ser' share preterite forms. 'Ellos fueron'.

questions.prompt: Conjugate 'dormir' for 'tú' in the present tense.
questions.tricks: 'Dormir' is a stem-changing verb (o→ue): yo duermo, tú duermes.

questions.prompt: What is the 'vosotros' form of 'hablar' in the present tense?
questions.tricks: 'Vosotros habláis' — vosotros form adds '-áis' for -ar verbs.

questions.prompt: Conjugate 'tener' for 'yo' in the present tense.
questions.tricks: 'Yo tengo' — 'tener' has an irregular 'yo' form.

questions.prompt: What is the future tense of 'hablar' for 'yo'?
questions.tricks: Future tense: infinitive + ending. 'Yo hablaré'.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct form: 'Ayer nosotros ___ al cine.' (ir)
questions.tricks: Preterite of 'ir' for nosotros = 'fuimos'.

questions.prompt: What is the imperative (command) for 'tú' with 'hablar'?
questions.tricks: Affirmative tú command for -ar verbs = third person singular present: 'habla'.

questions.prompt: Conjugate 'poder' for 'ellos' in the present tense.
questions.tricks: 'Ellos pueden' — stem-changing (o→ue) but nosotros/vosotros keep 'o'.

questions.prompt: What is the 'yo' form of 'saber' in the present tense?
questions.tricks: 'Saber' has an irregular 'yo' form: 'yo sé'.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Ella ___ música clásica.' (escuchar, present)
questions.tricks: 'Ella escucha' — third person singular of -ar verb.

questions.prompt: What is the conditional tense of 'comer' for 'nosotros'?
questions.tricks: Conditional: infinitive + conditional ending. 'Nosotros comeríamos'.

questions.prompt: Which sentence uses the subjunctive correctly?
questions.tricks: 'Esperar que' triggers the subjunctive mood: 'Espero que ella venga'.

questions.prompt: What is the Spanish word for 500?
questions.tricks: Quinientos = 500. Each hundred has a specific form in Spanish.

questions.prompt: How do you say 1,000 in Spanish?
questions.tricks: 'Mil' = 1,000. 'Cien' = 100; 'ciento' is used for 101–199.

questions.prompt: How do you write 352 in Spanish?
questions.tricks: Hundreds are written as one word (trescientos), no 'y' between hundreds and tens.

questions.prompt: How do you correctly say 99,000 in Spanish?
questions.tricks: Thousands in Spanish never pluralize: 'noventa y nueve mil', not 'miles'.

questions.prompt: A store bill reads 'doscientos cuarenta y siete euros.' What is the amount?
questions.tricks: Doscientos = 200, cuarenta = 40, siete = 7 → 247.

questions.prompt: What is 'cuatro mil setecientos treinta y ocho' in digits?
questions.tricks: Cuatro mil = 4,000; setecientos = 700; treinta y ocho = 38 → 4,738.

questions.prompt: What is 'cinco mil doscientos cuarenta y uno' in digits?
questions.tricks: Cinco mil = 5,000; doscientos = 200; cuarenta y uno = 41 → 5,241.

questions.prompt: What is the Spanish word for 700?
questions.tricks: Setecientos = 700.

questions.prompt: How do you write 1,245 in Spanish?
questions.tricks: Mil + doscientos + cuarenta y cinco. No 'y' after 'mil' or hundreds.

questions.prompt: A receipt reads 'cuatrocientos ochenta y seis euros.' What is the amount?
questions.tricks: Cuatrocientos = 400, ochenta = 80, seis = 6 → 486.

questions.prompt: How do you correctly say 15,000 in Spanish?
questions.tricks: 'Mil' does not pluralize: 'quince mil'.

questions.prompt: How do you say '21' in Spanish?
questions.tricks: 21 = 'veintiuno' (one word, no space). 21–29 are all one word.

questions.prompt: How do you say '100' in Spanish?
questions.tricks: 100 = 'cien' (used alone); 'ciento' is used for 101–199.

questions.prompt: How do you say '101' in Spanish?
questions.tricks: 101 = 'ciento uno'. 'Cien' becomes 'ciento' for 101–199.

questions.prompt: What is 'doscientos' in digits?
questions.tricks: Doscientos = 200.

questions.prompt: How do you say '1,000,000' in Spanish?
How do you say '16' in Spanish?
questions.tricks: 16 = 'dieciséis' (one word, with accent on the é).

questions.prompt: How do you say '200' when counting feminine nouns (e.g., 'doscientas páginas')?
questions.tricks: Hundreds agree in gender: 'doscientas' for feminine nouns.

questions.prompt: What is 'noventa' in digits?
questions.tricks: Noventa = 90.

questions.prompt: How do you say '50' in Spanish?
questions.tricks: Cincuenta = 50.

questions.prompt: What is 'treinta y tres' in digits?
questions.tricks: Treinta = 30, tres = 3 → 33.

questions.sectionLabel: Section 6: Days of the Week (Días de la Semana)
questions.prompt: How do you say 'Monday' in Spanish?
questions.tricks: Lunes = Monday.

questions.sectionLabel: Section 6: Days of the Week (Días de la Semana)
questions.prompt: How do you say 'Friday' in Spanish?
questions.tricks: Viernes = Friday.

questions.sectionLabel: Section 6: Days of the Week (Días de la Semana)
questions.prompt: Which day comes after 'miércoles' (Wednesday)?
questions.tricks: Jueves (Thursday) comes after miércoles (Wednesday).

questions.sectionLabel: Section 6: Days of the Week (Días de la Semana)
questions.prompt: What is 'el fin de semana' in English?
questions.tricks: 'Fin de semana' = weekend (Saturday and Sunday).

questions.sectionLabel: Section 6: Days of the Week (Días de la Semana)
questions.prompt: How do you say 'Sunday' in Spanish?
questions.tricks: Domingo = Sunday.

questions.sectionLabel: Section 6: Days of the Week (Días de la Semana)
questions.prompt: Which of the following is 'Tuesday' in Spanish?
questions.tricks: Martes = Tuesday.

questions.sectionLabel: Section 6: Days of the Week (Días de la Semana)
questions.prompt: In Spanish-speaking countries, which day is typically considered the first day of the week?
questions.tricks: Lunes (Monday) is considered the first day of the week in Spanish-speaking countries.

questions.sectionLabel: Section 6: Days of the Week (Días de la Semana)
questions.prompt: How do you say 'Today is Wednesday' in Spanish?
questions.tricks: Miércoles = Wednesday. 'Hoy es miércoles'.

questions.sectionLabel: Section 6: Days of the Week (Días de la Semana)
questions.prompt: How do you say 'Thursday' in Spanish?
questions.tricks: Jueves = Thursday.

questions.sectionLabel: Section 6: Days of the Week (Días de la Semana)
questions.prompt: How do you say 'Saturday' in Spanish?
questions.tricks: Sábado = Saturday.

questions.sectionLabel: Section 6: Days of the Week (Días de la Semana)
questions.prompt: How do you say 'on Mondays' (habitually) in Spanish?
questions.tricks: Habitual days use the definite plural article: 'los lunes'.

questions.sectionLabel: Section 6: Days of the Week (Días de la Semana)
questions.prompt: What is the abbreviation for 'miércoles' in a Spanish calendar?
questions.tricks: Miércoles is abbreviated as 'Mi' or 'X' in some calendars.

questions.sectionLabel: Section 6: Days of the Week (Días de la Semana)
questions.prompt: How do you say 'last Tuesday' in Spanish?
questions.tricks: 'Pasado' means last/past: 'el martes pasado'.

questions.sectionLabel: Section 6: Days of the Week (Días de la Semana)
questions.prompt: How do you say 'next Friday' in Spanish?
questions.tricks: 'Próximo' means next: 'el próximo viernes'.

questions.sectionLabel: Section 6: Days of the Week (Días de la Semana)
questions.prompt: Which sentence means 'I work every Saturday'?
questions.tricks: 'Todos los sábados' = every Saturday.

questions.prompt: What is the Spanish word for 'red'?
questions.tricks: Rojo = red.

questions.prompt: How do you say 'blue' in Spanish?
questions.tricks: Azul = blue.

questions.prompt: What color is 'amarillo'?
questions.tricks: Amarillo = yellow.

questions.prompt: What is 'negro' in English?
questions.tricks: Negro = black.

questions.prompt: How do you say 'green' in Spanish?
questions.tricks: Verde = green.

questions.prompt: What color is 'blanco'?
questions.tricks: Blanco = white.

questions.prompt: How do you say 'orange' in Spanish?
questions.tricks: Naranja = orange.

questions.prompt: What is the Spanish word for 'purple'?
questions.tricks: Morado = purple.

questions.prompt: What does 'rosa' mean in English?
questions.tricks: Rosa = pink.

questions.prompt: What is the Spanish word for 'brown'?
questions.tricks: Marrón = brown (also 'café' in Latin America).

questions.prompt: 'La camisa es ___'. (The shirt is grey.)
questions.tricks: Gris = grey/gray.

questions.prompt: Which sentence is grammatically correct? (El cielo / azul)
questions.tricks: Adjectives agree with the noun: 'cielo' is masculine singular → 'azul' (azul is invariable for gender).

questions.prompt: How do you say 'the red car' in Spanish? (El coche ___)
questions.tricks: 'Coche' is masculine singular → 'rojo'.

questions.prompt: Which is correct? 'The yellow flowers' (Las flores ___)
questions.tricks: 'Flores' is feminine plural → 'amarillas'.

questions.prompt: How do you say 'a gray cat' in Spanish?
questions.tricks: 'Gato' is masculine singular → 'un gato gris'. 'Gris' is invariable for gender.

questions.prompt: Which color means 'light blue' in Spanish?
questions.tricks: 'Claro' = light, so 'azul claro' = light blue.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'El cielo es ___ hoy.' (cloudy / gray)
questions.tricks: A cloudy sky looks gray → 'gris'.

questions.prompt: How do you describe 'the green trees' in Spanish?
questions.tricks: 'Árboles' is masculine plural → 'los árboles verdes'.

questions.prompt: What is 'morado oscuro' in English?
questions.tricks: 'Oscuro' = dark, 'morado' = purple → dark purple.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct sentence: 'She is wearing a white dress.'
questions.tricks: 'Vestido' is masculine → 'un vestido blanco'.

questions.prompt: What color is associated with envy in the Spanish expression 'verde de envidia'?
questions.tricks: 'Verde de envidia' = green with envy.

questions.prompt: How do you say 'the blue and white flag' in Spanish?
questions.tricks: 'Bandera' is feminine → both adjectives are feminine: 'azul' (invariable) and 'blanca'.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct translation for 10:30.
questions.tricks: 'Y media' means 'half past'. 10:30 → 'Son las diez y media'. Note: plural 'son' for 10.

questions.prompt: How do you say 'It is one o'clock' in Spanish?
questions.tricks: Only 1 o'clock uses 'Es la una' (singular). All other hours use 'Son las...'.

questions.prompt: ¿Cómo se dice 'It is 1:15' en español?
questions.tricks: 1:15 → 'Es la una y cuarto.' (singular 'Es la' for 1 o'clock).

questions.prompt: How do you say 'It is midnight'?

choices.text: Son las doce de la tarde.
Son las doce de la mañana.

questions.tricks: 'Es la medianoche' or 'Son las doce de la noche'.

questions.prompt: ¿Qué hora es? (3:00)
questions.tricks: 3:00 → 'Son las tres en punto' (en punto = exactly).

questions.prompt: ¿Qué hora es? (4:45)

choices.text: Son las cuatro y cuarenta y cinco

questions.tricks: 4:45 = 15 minutes to 5 → 'Son las cinco menos cuarto'.

questions.prompt: What is the correct way to say '8:30' in Spanish?

choices.text: Son las ocho de la media

questions.tricks: 'Media' (half) is feminine → 'y media'. 8:30 → 'Son las ocho y media'.

questions.prompt: ¿Qué significa 'Son las nueve menos cuarto'?
questions.tricks: 'Menos cuarto' = minus 15 minutes. 9:00 − 0:15 = 8:45.

questions.prompt: Son las cinco menos cuarto. ¿Qué hora es?
¿Qué hora es: 'Son las diez y veinte'?
questions.tricks: 'Y veinte' means 'plus twenty minutes'. 10:20.

questions.prompt: ¿Cómo se dice '7:15' en español?
questions.tricks: 7:15 → 'Son las siete y cuarto' ('cuarto' = quarter).

questions.prompt: ¿Cómo se dice '1:30' en español?
questions.tricks: 1:30 → 'Es la una y media'. Note singular 'Es la' for 1 o'clock.

questions.prompt: ¿Cómo se dice '2:45' en español?

choices.text: Son las dos y cuarenta y cinco
Es la dos y cuarenta y cinco

questions.tricks: 2:45 = 15 minutes to 3 → 'Son las tres menos cuarto'.
questions.sourceIssue: The PDF marks option C ('Son las dos menos cuarto') as correct, but the explanation says 2:45 should be 'Son las tres menos cuarto'.

questions.prompt: ¿Cómo se dice '8:50' en español?
questions.tricks: 8:50 = 10 minutes to 9 → 'Son las nueve menos diez'.

questions.prompt: Son las seis y siete. ¿Qué hora es?
questions.tricks: 'Son las seis y siete' = 6:07.
